description: A designed vertical 'headline/news-quote' graphic marking Labor Day 2025. The upper half is a photograph of three smiling people standing shoulder to shoulder in front of a corrugated-metal garage/shop wall: a young man at left in a blue 'TowHaul' t-shirt, ballcap, glasses, and beard; a woman in the center with long dark hair in a dark blue top (presented as the Labor Secretary or an official); and a young man at right in a blue 'TowHaul' polo and cap with sunglasses on the brim. A blue gradient washes the lower portion of the photo. Overlaid text: a small blue pill badge reading 'LABOR DAY 2025', then a large white headline in mixed italic-serif and bold sans 'Making America SKILLED AGAIN!'. Below, a white rounded box shows the masthead 'The Washington Times' (blackletter) and a bold headline quote: 'Labor Department awards grants to expand Trump goal of one million apprenticeships'. A small DOL seal appears centered at the very bottom. The design pairs friendly imagery of young tradespeople with a supportive news headline to promote the administration's apprenticeship/skilled-trades agenda. Target audience: general public, workers, and the base, framing DOL grant programs as restoring American skilled labor.
notable_text: LABOR DAY 2025; Making America SKILLED AGAIN!; The Washington Times; Labor Department awards grants to expand Trump goal of one million apprenticeships
